candidate: L2 SHORT-TERM REVERSAL -- contrarian (long past-losers / short past-winners), 1-month and 1-week formation=hold, liquid universe, D-207 realistic cost
hypothesis: Short-horizon past returns REVERSE (Jegadeesh 1990, Lehmann 1990; Bildik-Gulay contrarian for BIST): recent losers out-return recent winners over the next week/month. Contrarian portfolio = LONG bottom past-return tercile, SHORT top past-return tercile.

REV_1W.rebalance: weekly (last trading day of each ISO week)

specs_FROZEN.note: TWO canonical specs declared up front (NOT a swept grid). Formation return = compounded clipped daily return over the trailing formation_days ENDING AT the rebalance close (incl. day t); enter at day-t close, hold forward to the next rebalance. Look-ahead-safe.

construction.signal: past formation-window return (LOW = loser = LONG; HIGH = winner = SHORT) -- contrarian
construction.long_leg: bottom tercile of past return (losers), EW -> deployable long-only read vs EW-full
construction.short_leg: top tercile (winners), EW -> academic loser-minus-winner spread

benchmark: EW-full per-period (honest bar). PRIMARY = geometric per-period relative (carry-immune).

cost.model: D-207 per-stock realistic round-trip (d204.per_stock_cost_panel quoted-primary + d204_basket_net_series). Report breakeven + realized bps + turnover. NOTE: realistic spread cost is the direct antidote to bid-ask-bounce illusion -- the breakeven test asks whether the reversal exceeds the spread it is mechanically built on.

honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.summary: Short-term reversal is academically STRONG on BIST (Bildik-Gulay) BUT microcap-concentrated and contaminated by BID-ASK BOUNCE; it is also HIGH TURNOVER (weekly/monthly rebalance, buying losers = often the widest-spread names).
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.most_likely_verdict: COST-WALL. The contrarian edge likely exists gross (especially REV_1W on ALL/illiquid) but is eaten by realistic spread cost on the liquid universe -- the edge ~ the spread it is built on. The L-S spread may be significant GROSS but breakeven < realistic cost. Long-loser-tercile vs EW-full may even be negative on liquid (losers are often the names that keep falling among large caps). NOT expecting a deployable edge; cheap, closes a never-isolated-tested gap. NO celebration.
honest_expectation_DECLARED_BEFORE_RESUL.cross_check: L3 found K=1 LIQUID high-minus-low-SUE significantly NEGATIVE -- a reversal hint. L2 tests reversal DIRECTLY and independently (reversal was pre-declared in the lab AGENDA, not derived from the L3 residual).

keep_bar_FROZEN.definition: TRADEABLE-aday requires, on the LIQUID universe, the long-loser-tercile (deployable, market-relative) for AT LEAST ONE spec with ALL of: (1) net-after-realistic-cost relative mean > 0, (2) NW |t| >= 2.0, (3) regime sign-stable (2022-01 split). The loser-minus-winner spread is corroboration; the deployable gate is long-only.
keep_bar_FROZEN.else: cost-wall or significance-wall -> archived. No grid-sweep over formation/hold (p-hacking forbidden).
